query: Make a workflow that reviews each changed file on this branch for bugs, then adversarially verifies each finding as soon as its review lands.
expected_behavior: Invokes the workflow-creator skill before writing any file
Writes a .js file under .claude/workflows/ whose first statement is export const meta = {...} as a pure literal with name and description and no backticks
Uses pipeline() for the review-then-verify stages (no barrier between them) with schema on every cross-stage agent() call
Runs scripts/validate-workflow.mjs on the file and it exits 0
Runs scripts/dry-run.mjs on the file and it reports parseOk=true ran=true deterministic=true

query: I want a repeatable monthly job: for each of our planning folders, one agent audits it and a second agent fixes what the audit found, all folders in parallel but never more than a dozen agents at once.
expected_behavior: Invokes the workflow-creator skill even though the word workflow never appears
Produces a fan-out or per-folder pipeline shape with an explicit concurrency governor (slot scheduler or bounded pool), never an unbounded parallel()
parallel() calls, if any, receive thunk arrays, never bare promises
Both bundled scripts pass on the produced file

query: My workflow file fails to launch with an error saying meta must be the first statement and a template literal was found. Fix it.
expected_behavior: Moves or rebuilds the meta block as the first statement, converts template literals and backticks inside meta to plain single-quoted strings
Re-runs scripts/validate-workflow.mjs to prove the fix

query: Run the rebuild workflow on libs/python/geometry.
expected_behavior: Does NOT invoke the workflow-creator skill
Launches the existing workflow by scriptPath via the Workflow tool with the target as args
Captures the run ID for resume

query: Spawn one subagent to grep the repo for TODO comments and summarize them.
expected_behavior: Does NOT invoke the workflow-creator skill and does NOT author a workflow file
Uses a single Agent tool call for the one-off task
